title: MarketPal Strategy Config
description: YAML strategy config for mpal-cli. Built-in strategies should use a defaults profile plus visible scoring and sizing knobs. Advanced full configs may still specify event guardrails, long-only behavior, unscored-holding protection, and backtest assumptions explicitly.

$schema.description: Optional editor hint pointing to this schema.

id.description: Stable strategy identifier, for example engine_weekly_swing_v1.

defaults.description: Named profile for advanced defaults. swing_v1 enables the standard event guardrail; basic_v1 keeps event guardrails disabled. Both profiles apply long-only, protected unscored holdings, and standard backtest assumptions.

description.description: Short explanation of what the strategy is for.

when_to_use.description: Human guidance for choosing this strategy.

cadence.description: Expected review cadence, for example weekly or daily_or_weekly.

approved.description: Whether scheduled or agent-assisted runs may use this strategy.

momentum_weight.description: Weight of momentum in the final signal score. All scoring weights must sum to 1.

profile_weight.description: Weight of aggregate profile/QVM score in the final signal score. All scoring weights must sum to 1.

quality_weight.description: Optional weight of the QVM quality component. Use this for quality-focused strategies instead of relying only on aggregate profile score.

value_weight.description: Optional weight of the QVM value component. Higher scores indicate more attractive valuation.

reversion_weight.description: Optional weight of the mean-reversion setup score. The v1 score favors a meaningful but not extreme drawdown from the one-year high.

min_buy_score.description: Minimum final score for new buys and top-ups.

min_hold_score.description: Minimum final score before an existing holding becomes a reduction candidate.

event_guardrail.description: Advanced override. Built-in swing strategies use defaults: swing_v1 instead of specifying this block.

enabled.description: Whether recent article event scores can veto or boost signals.

event_veto_score.description: Event score at or below which a candidate is vetoed.

event_boost_score.description: Event score at or above which a candidate receives a boost.

event_boost_amount.description: Amount added to the final score after a positive event boost.

long_only.description: Advanced override. Built-in strategies get long-only behavior from their defaults profile.

max_positions.description: Maximum number of active holdings after planning.

max_position_pct.description: Maximum target weight for one holding.

min_trade_value.description: Minimum estimated trade value needed for a proposed trade.

rebalance.description: Planner cadence label, for example daily or weekly.

listing_region_tilt.description: Optional soft starter-selection preference. When preferred exposure is low and scores are close, the planner may prefer this listing region.

profile.description: Optional shorthand for fixed risk caps: turnover_budget_pct, max_single_trade_pct, and starter_position_pct. Explicit numeric fields override the profile. These caps still clamp fractional Kelly sizing.

turnover_budget_pct.description: Maximum total absolute trade weight per run.

max_single_trade_pct.description: Maximum absolute target-weight change for one trade.

starter_position_pct.description: Default target weight for a new starter position.

max_new_positions_per_run.description: Maximum number of new buy positions per run.

cash_buffer_pct.description: Minimum portfolio cash weight to preserve.

protect_unscored_holdings.description: Advanced override. Built-in strategies get this from their defaults profile.

sizing_method.description: Easy sizing switch. Defaults to fixed. Set to fractional_kelly to use conservative internal Kelly defaults from Markov edge. Kelly computes a desired target; risk.profile and explicit risk controls still clamp the final trade.

kelly_fraction.description: Easy fractional Kelly tuning knob. Fraction of the confidence-shrunk Kelly estimate to use when sizing_method is fractional_kelly. Defaults to 0.25; common conservative values are 0.25 and 0.50.

kelly_min_edge.description: Advanced override. Minimum raw Kelly edge required before a Kelly target is usable. Defaults to 0.

kelly_max_fraction.description: Advanced override. Maximum target weight allowed from the Kelly overlay before standard portfolio and trade caps are applied. Defaults to 0.05.

kelly_default_payoff_ratio.description: Advanced override. Payoff ratio used in the generalized Kelly calculation when sizing_method is fractional_kelly. Defaults to 1.0.

kelly_min_confidence.description: Advanced override. Minimum Markov confidence required for Kelly sizing. Defaults to 0.25.

kelly_min_sample_count.description: Advanced override. Minimum Markov sample count required for Kelly sizing. Defaults to 30.

kelly_missing_edge_policy.description: Advanced override. Behavior when Markov edge data is absent under fractional_kelly sizing. fixed falls back to fixed sizing; skip rejects the candidate. Defaults to fixed.

backtest.description: Advanced override. Built-in strategies get standard backtest assumptions from their defaults profile.

fee_bps.description: Backtest fee assumption in basis points.

slippage_bps.description: Backtest slippage assumption in basis points.
